dailyJudgementSnippet: HN's AI mood was harsh for the big lab-branded products: OpenAI was dragged by the Sam Altman trust story, Anthropic was hit even harder by Claude Code 'silent nerf' complaints, and Microsoft Copilot only got a smaller UX knock. Google's Gemma had the least-damaging, mostly technical mixed discussion, making google_gemini the relative winner [E1][E2][E4][E5].
winnerExplanation: google_gemini wins mostly by staying out of the blast radius. The only meaningful Google AI signal was limited, mixed technical discussion around Gemma local demos, with some appreciation for what the model enables and some complaints that it is heavyweight [E5]. By contrast, OpenAI and Anthropic were dominated by sharp distrust/quality complaints [E1][E2], and Microsoft Copilot's only clear signal was negative [E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: Mostly strongly negative. The Altman trust investigation heavily colored HN discussion against OpenAI, and a small positive comparison on web-enabled chat was nowhere near enough to offset it [E1][E6].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Net strongly negative. Claude Code backlash over alleged hidden reasoning-depth cuts and subscription behavior dominated, though a separate discussion still showed some real affection for Claude Code as a practical coding tool [E2][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Light mixed/neutral coverage. Gemma drew some technically positive interest for local use and fine-tuning, but the discussion was limited and included weight/performance complaints [E5].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Mildly negative and sparse. HN used Microsoft's Bing/Copilot chat rollout as an example of bad execution on a promising AI product idea [E4].

evidence.summary: Front-page story asking whether Sam Altman can be trusted; top comments praise the reporting and include a serious allegation that Altman's bias affected hiring at OpenAI, creating strong distrust around OpenAI leadership.

Claude Code issue says February updates made it unusable for complex engineering; comments allege Anthropic silently reduced reasoning depth and hid it with thinking redaction, with some users saying they will cancel.

Vibe-coding discussion includes comments arguing Claude Code's messy internals do not negate its success and saying it is genuinely useful for refactors, duplication cleanup, and other code-quality work.
In a Microsoft GUI-strategy thread, a commenter recalls Bing/AI chat as a squandered opportunity because Microsoft blocked chats after only a few messages and failed to make the UX truly usable.

Gemma E2B local realtime demo gets praise as promising workshop-assistant/local-AI tech and as easy to fine-tune, but another comment says Gemma 4/E2B is too heavyweight compared with smaller alternatives.
That same Bing AI chat comparison says OpenAI later implemented web-integrated chat properly, giving OpenAI a small positive contrast mention.
